issue.weather: Cloudy w/ chance of patch notes

ticker.text: Pragmata — 9.2/10. Hugh and Diana are gaming's best new duo
Hades II on PS5/Xbox — 9.5/10. 120fps, day-one Game Pass, still perfect
Vampire Crawlers drops Tuesday — $9.99, day one Game Pass
Saros launches April 30 — five years to the day after Returnal
GTA 6 confirmed for November 19 — Rockstar is in its release fiscal year

lead.title: Capcom Waited Six Years to Deliver This. It Was Worth Every Second.
lead.dek: After delay after delay, Pragmata landed with a 9.2 and 97% positive on Steam. Hugh and Diana are gaming's best new duo, and the hacking-shooting combat is the freshest thing in the genre in years. Our full verdict is in.
lead.author: Romello Morris

poll.question: What are you playing this week?

calendar.title: Vampire Crawlers
calendar.sub: Full Launch · Day One Game Pass

calendar.title: Saros
calendar.sub: PS5 Exclusive · Deluxe Early Access Apr 28

spotlight.title: The Cartoon Detective Shooter Nobody Saw Coming
spotlight.blurb: Mouse: P.I. For Hire renders an entire city in 1930s rubber-hose animation and wraps a boomer shooter inside it. It's a debut that has no right being this confident — and at $39.99, it's the easiest recommendation of the week.

comments.name: Marcus T.
comments.body: Pragmata 9.2 is deserved. The hacking mechanic genuinely changes how you think about the gunplay — Diana isn't a gimmick, she's the whole game.

comments.name: Jade W.
comments.body: Hades II at 120fps on PS5 is something else. Console players were sleeping on what PC had. It's not hyperbole to call this one of the best games ever made.

comments.name: Devon K.
comments.body: Mouse P.I. is my sleeper of the year. Nobody was talking about it two weeks ago. At $9.99 it would be a steal — at $39.99 it's still an easy yes.

comments.name: Aisha M.
comments.body: Vampire Crawlers tomorrow. Poncle going from Vampire Survivors to a first-person deckbuilder blobber is the most unhinged pivot in indie dev history and I respect it completely.
